items.label: Most data structures reduce to directed graphs + traversal rules
Problem difficulty is often a function of representation, not inherent complexity
Approximate GI (WL, graph kernels) may suffice for pattern recognition
Hybrid representation rejected — complexity absorbed upward through layer hierarchy
Rule space bloat is lesser evil than data space bloat
Library entries must be rule-invariant pairs, not standalone subgraphs
Library entry requires label-correlated GA-convergent production, not mere frequency
